column1: Data download for the Food Security in the U.S. Interactive Charts and Highlights (Tableau Data Visualizations)

1. This file contains six tabs with statistics on food security in the United States, calculated by USDA's Economic Research Service (ERS). The data source for these statistics is the Current Population Survey Food Security Supplement (CPS-FSS) from the U.S. Department of Commerce, Bureau of the Census and sponsored by USDA, ERS.
2. For more information on food security definitions and measurement, see the "Food Security in the U.S." topic page on the USDA, ERS website.
3. Most of the statistics in this file were previously reported in USDA's annual report series Household Food Security in the United States, available on the USDA, ERS website.

The separate tabs in this data download include statistics that correspond to the interactive charts on the USDA, ERS website and are found in the tables in the annual report series Household Food Security in the United States. The specific chart and tables that match each tab are described below.

The tab labeled "Food security all households" includes statistics on food security for all U.S. households from 2001 to 2024 that are presented in the interactive chart, "Trends in U.S. food security," and in table 2 and 1a in the annual reports Household Food Security in the United States. Source: USDA, Economic Research Service, using data from the Current Population Survey Food Security Supplement, 20012024.

The tab labeled "Food security HHs with children" (HH=households) includes statistics on food security for households with children from 2008 to 2024 that are presented in the interactive chart, "Trends in food insecurity in U.S. households with children," and in table 3 in the annual reports Household Food Security in the United States. Source: USDA, Economic Research Service, using data from the Current Population Survey Food Security Supplement, 20082024.

The tab labeled "Child food security trends" includes statistics on food security for households with children and food insecurity among children from 1998 to 2024 that are presented in the interactive chart, "Trends in food insecurity in U.S. households with children," and in tables 3 and 1b in the annual reports Household Food Security in the United States. Note that the interactive chart displays only the years 2001 to 2024. Source: USDA, Economic Research Service, using data from the Current Population Survey Food Security Supplement, 19982024.

The tab labeled "Edu-employment-disability" includes statistics from 2017 to 2024 on household education, employment and disability that are presented in the interactive chart, "Prevalence of food insecurity and very low food security by  education, employment, and disability status." These statistics are not presented in the annual food security reports. Source: USDA, Economic Research Service, using data from the Current Population Survey Food Security Supplement, 20172024.

The tab labeled "Food security by State" includes State-level food security statistics in 3-year averages from the 20062008 period to the 20222024 period that are presented in the interactive chart, "Food Security in the United States: How Do States Compare," and in Tables 4 and 5 in the annual reports Household Food Security in the United States (data for 3 years were combined to provide more reliable Statelevel statistics). Source: USDA, Economic Research Service, using data from the Current Population Survey Food Security Supplement, 20062024.
Note: Not applicable = A subcategory or sub-subcategory is not applicable to the larger category. N/A = Not reported; fewer than 10 households in the survey with this characteristic.
Source: USDA, Economic Research Service using data from U.S. Department of Commerce, Bureau of the Census, Current Population Survey Food Security Supplements.
